title: phoradendron flavescens subsp. macrophyllum
intro: Phoradendron flavescens subsp. macrophyllum, also known as the "strangler fig", is a type of parasitic plant that belongs to the family Santalaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Phoradendron flavescens subsp. macrophyllum are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them as a tincture to create teas, infusions, or ointments for topical application.
